Title: Add recurring invoice scheduler
Body: As a freelancer I want to mark an invoice as recurring (weekly/monthly/quarterly) so the system automatically creates and sends the next invoice when the period expires, without me logging in.

- The new invoice copies line items, client, and currency from the template
- An email is sent via SendGrid with the PDF attached
- Job is idempotent — running twice on the same day must not double-create

FEATURE REQUEST — Title: Optimistic lock on itinerary day blocks to prevent silent overwrites

When two editors move activities on the same day simultaneously, the last write silently wins and one user's changes are lost without notification. Implement optimistic locking using a version counter on each day_block row. The PATCH endpoint should accept an expectedVersion field; if the DB version differs, return 409 Conflict with the current server state so the client can rebase.

Acceptance criteria:
- day_blocks table gains a version integer column (default 1, increments on each update)
- PATCH /api/trips/:tripId/days/:dayId returns 409 with { conflict: true, serverState } when version mismatch
- Successful update increments version and returns new version to client
- Socket.IO broadcasts version change to all collaborators in the trip room
- Tests cover concurrent update scenario returning 409

Labels: enhancement, collaboration, data-integrity

FEATURE REQUEST
Title: Add automatic driver reassignment when driver goes offline mid-delivery
Body: When a driver's heartbeat stops being received for more than 90 seconds during an active delivery, the system should automatically mark the delivery as REASSIGNMENT_PENDING, attempt to find the nearest available driver within a configurable radius (default 15 km), and if one is found, atomically reassign the delivery and emit a fleet.delivery.reassigned NATS event. If no driver is found within the radius, escalate to fleet.delivery.escalation. The original driver's status should be set to OFFLINE_MID_DELIVERY for ops review. All reassignment attempts should be logged to a reassignment_audit table with timestamps, original driver ID, new driver ID, and reason.
Labels: enhancement, dispatch, reliability

Title: Add automatic driver reassignment when driver goes offline mid-delivery
Body: When a driver's heartbeat stops being received for more than 90 seconds during an active delivery, the system should automatically mark the delivery as REASSIGNMENT_PENDING, attempt to find the nearest available driver within a configurable radius (default 15 km), and if one is found, atomically reassign the delivery and emit a fleet.delivery.reassigned NATS event. If no driver is found within the radius, escalate to fleet.delivery.escalation. The original driver's status should be set to OFFLINE_MID_DELIVERY for ops review. All reassignment attempts should be logged to a reassignment_audit table with timestamps, original driver ID, new driver ID, and reason.
Labels: enhancement, dispatch, reliability

  "body": "**Describe the bug**\nEmployees using devices with a clock drift of more than ~1 second occasionally see course progress reset to zero on their mobile app after a sync. }
